behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names the file (packages/excalidraw/clipboard.ts), the function (copyTextToSystemClipboard), the variable (plainTextEntry), the change from let to const, the return statement placement inside the if(clipboardEvent) block, the removal of 'plainTextEntry = undefined' and replacement with 'return;', and the test commands. All assert'd specifics in tests (file path, variable name, patterns) are discoverable from instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the exact fix: 'use const not let', 'move return; inside the if (clipboardEvent) block', 'remove the old code that set entry to undefined and replace with return;'. This is a direct specification of the solution, not just a description of broken behavior.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on exact implementation details: test_const_plainTextEntry_not_let checks for literal 'const plainTextEntry = entries.find', test_return_after_navigator_clipboard_writeText uses regex matching exact pattern 'await navigator\.clipboard\.writeText\(plainTextEntry\[1\]\);\s*return;', test_no_plainTextEntry_reassignment counts exact number of assignments. An alternative correct fix (e.g., using a different variable name or restructuring the logic differently) would fail these text-match

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_const_plainTextEntry_not_let, test_return_inside_clipboardEvent_block, test_return_after_navigator_clipboard_writeText, test_no_plainTextEntry_reassignment) are ALL pure text/regex matching on file contents with no execution or behavioral testing. While pass-to-pass tests do run subprocesses (typecheck, lint, unit tests), the core f2p tests only do 'assert X in open(path).read()'.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests only check text patterns in the source file. A stub or no-op implementation wouldn't pass, but these tests don't verify actual runtime behavior — they verify specific text edits were made. An agent could make the exact text changes without understanding the behavioral fix, and all f2p tests would pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All three required changes from instruction.md (const vs let, return inside if block, return after writeText instead of setting undefined) are tested, plus typecheck, lint, prettier, and clipboard unit/integration tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive function names (test_const_plainTextEntry_not_let, test_return_inside_clipboardEvent_block, etc.) and include docstrings indicating fail-to-pass vs pass-to-pass classification.
